title: Principal AI Engineer
description:
• Architect GenAI platforms and systems with modular design,
scalable pipelines, and secure LLM integrations • Define AI
security, privacy, and governance standards — including
model context management, output safety, and explainability
frameworks • Drive the adoption of agent orchestration
frameworks and protocols for multi-agent collaboration and
automation • Evaluate emerging tools and abstract them into
capability-driven design patterns — e.g., orchestration,
vectorization, summarization, or autonomous reasoning •
Partner with platform, security, and product leads to shape
long-term AI direction across Ecolab Digital Center • Build
reusable components (SDKs, prompt libraries, orchestration
templates) to accelerate solution delivery • Provide
technical mentorship to Lead and Senior Engineers; elevate
engineering craft through code quality reviews, architecture
walkthroughs, and tech talks • Champion responsible AI
practices and ensure model deployments align with enterprise
risk, compliance, and ethical standards

Required Qualifications:

• 8+ years of experience in AI/ML/GenAI systems, with 3+
years in technical architecture or staff-level engineering
roles • Demonstrated expertise in building LLM-integrated
systems, embedding pipelines, and agentic workflows in
production environments • Deep understanding of prompt
optimization, vector search, context management, and multi-
step task orchestration • Experience with cloud-native
delivery (Azure preferred), containerized workloads, and
scalable microservices • Strong grasp of model safety, cost
optimization, and observability in GenAI applications
responsibilities: Architect GenAI platforms and systems with modular design, scalable pipelines, and secure LLM integrations
Define AI security, privacy, and governance standards including model context management, output safety, and explainability frameworks
Drive adoption of agent orchestration frameworks and protocols for multi-agent collaboration and automation
Evaluate emerging tools and abstract them into capability-driven design patterns for orchestration, vectorization, summarization, and autonomous reasoning
Build reusable components including SDKs, prompt libraries, and orchestration templates to accelerate solution delivery
Provide technical mentorship to Lead and Senior Engineers through code reviews, architecture walkthroughs, and tech talks
use_cases: Enterprise GenAI platform for scalable AI solutions
Enterprise AI governance and compliance framework
LLM-powered applications with vector search and retrieval
Responsible AI deployment with safety and observability
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is directly building and architecting GenAI
platforms and systems with LLM integrations, agent
orchestration frameworks, and multi-agent collaboration. The
responsibilities involve hands-on work with prompt
optimization, vector search, context management, and
deploying LLM-integrated systems in production environments,
which aligns with ai-first classification.
